description:
## Bug Report

**Category:** Functional
**Priority:** P1 (High)
**Component:** #pt-run-ggl-sanity button in Game Testing tab

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Navigate to http://localhost:4000
2. Click the Game Testing tab
3. Click the "Run GGL Sanity" button

### Expected Behavior
The button should trigger a GGL sanity test run (similar to Run Selected or Run All).

### Actual Behavior
Nothing happens. The button is completely non-functional — no click handler is registered in gamepulse.js.

### Test Reference
- Test file: tests/validation/dashboard-gametesting-full.spec.ts
- Test name: Run GGL Sanity button has a click handler
- Describe block: Game Testing Tab — Integration Flow

### Technical Details
- Selector: #pt-run-ggl-sanity
- File: dashboard/public/js/gamepulse.js
- The string "pt-run-ggl-sanity" does not appear anywhere in gamepulse.js
- The button is defined in dashboard/public/index.html line 261 but has no corresponding event listener
title: [Functional] Run GGL Sanity button has no click handler

description:
## Bug Report

**Category:** API
**Priority:** P2 (Medium)
**Component:** GET /api/games/catalog endpoint

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Send GET request to http://localhost:4000/api/games/catalog
2. Inspect the JSON response body

### Expected Behavior
The endpoint should return a JSON array of game objects, consistent with the endpoint name /games/catalog.

### Actual Behavior
Returns { games: [...] } — an object wrapper with a 'games' property containing the array.
This is inconsistent with the endpoint semantics and forces clients to unwrap the response.

### Test Reference
- Test file: tests/validation/dashboard-gametesting-full.spec.ts
- Test name: GET /api/games/catalog returns valid catalog
- Describe block: Game Testing Tab — API Endpoints

### Technical Details
- Endpoint: GET /api/games/catalog
- File: dashboard/routes/api-routes.ts line 101
- Response shape: { games: CatalogGame[] } instead of CatalogGame[]
- Frontend gamepulse.js already handles the wrapper, but the API contract is ambiguous
title: [API] /api/games/catalog returns object wrapper instead of array

description: Steps 6-10 all report PASS with ~99.4% diff for Ammit Hellfire, but screenshots show NO actual interaction occurred. Reels never spun (same symbols), balance unchanged ($1,044.40), STAKE unchanged ($2.00), bet popup never opened, paytable never opened. Canvas fire/flame animations produce constant pixel diff that satisfies the changed threshold. Fix: use static-region comparison (bottom bar CREDIT/WIN/STAKE) or reel-area-only diff instead of full-canvas diff. Session: pt-1772690693944-i8c191. Screenshots: reports/pipeline-validation/ammitHellfireDesktop/
title: [Pipeline Infra] Canvas animation false-positive diff — affects all GGL animated games
